data.title: Telus commences up to C$600M cash tender offer for outstanding senior notes
data.description:
Communications technology company Telus (NYSE:TU) (TSX:T:CA) announced on Friday the commencement of an up to C$600M cash tender offer for certain series of outstanding senior notes.

TU shares were -0.62% pre-market to $15.92.

The notes are -

  • 3.95% Senior Notes, Series CAB due February, 2050 (Fixed Spread +145 basis points)
  • 4.10% Senior Notes, Series CAE due April, 2051 (Fixed Spread +145 basis points)
  • 2.05% Senior Notes, Series CAD due October, 2030 (Fixed Spread +70 basis points)
  • 4.40% Senior Notes, Series CU due January, 2046 (Fixed Spread +150 basis points)
  • 4.40% Senior Notes, Series CL due April, 2043 (Fixed Spread +150 basis points)
  • 2.85% Senior Notes, Series CAF due November, 2031 (Fixed Spread +90 basis points)
  • 4.70% Senior Notes, Series CW due March, 2048 (Fixed Spread +160 basis points)
  • 4.75% Senior Notes, Series CR due January, 2045 (Fixed Spread +160 basis points)

The consideration payable per each C$1,000 principal amount will be based on the applicable fixed spread, plus the applicable yield.

The offers will expire at 5:00 PM ET on June 27. The settlement date for the notes validly tendered is expected to be July 3.

The company said the maximum purchase amount may increase, decrease, or waived in its sole discretion.

data.title: SHARC Energy announces $1.5M convertible debenture private placement
data.description:
• SHARC Energy plans to raise up to $1.5M via a non-brokered private placement of unsecured convertible debentures.

  • The offering includes a 15% over-allotment option, potentially increasing total proceeds to $1.725M.

  • Debentures will bear 10% annual interest and mature 24 months from issuance, with a possible 12-month extension.

  • Conversion into common shares is available at $0.15/share, at the holder’s option.

  • Proceeds will support working capital as the company delivers SHARC and PIRANHA WET systems.

  • Source: Press release

data.title: Belgravia Hartford expands Bitcoin treasury with additional purchase
data.description:
• Belgravia Hartford Capital (OTCPK:BLGVF) purchased an additional 1.5316 BTC for $160,842.59 using cash-on-hand.

  • The average purchase price was $105,013.65 per BTC, inclusive of all fees.

  • The company’s total Bitcoin holdings now stand at 6.3932 BTC, with an average cost of $103,367.05 per BTC.

  • Purchases were made via Coinsquare’s regulated OTC desk and not through the $5M credit facility from Round13 DAF.

  • Belgravia plans to use the credit facility in the future when strategically appropriate.

  • Source: Press release

data.title: Gene editors steady despite latest leadership shake-up at FDA
data.description:
Shares of gene editing companies were largely steady in the premarket on Friday after the FDA’s Center for Biologics Research and Evaluation (CBER) faced another abrupt leadership transition following the sudden departure of its former head, Dr. Peter Marks, early this year.

According to multiple media reports, the director and deputy director of the CBER’s cell and gene therapy division were placed on administrative leave and escorted out of the agency following an all-hands meeting on Wednesday.

The sudden departures of Nicole Verdun, FDA’s Super Office Director of Therapeutic Products, and her deputy Rachael Anatol came weeks after new CBER head Dr. Vinay Prasad took over the division, replacing Marks, who was also forced out of the agency in March.

In a statement to the U.K. pharma publication Pink Sheet, the Health and Human Services (HHS) Department’s press secretary, Andrew Nixon, indicated that the views of Verdun and Anatol diverged from those of Dr. Prasad.

“Center directors deserve to be supported by managers that are aligned with aggressive goals to expeditiously advance therapeutics for rare diseases using the gold standard of science,” Nixon said.

Gene therapy developers sold off in May after the FDA Commissioner, Dr. Marty Makary, announced Dr. Prasad as the new CBER head.

A fierce critic of the FDA’s prior regulatory work, including the controversial approval of Elevidys, a gene therapy developed by Sarepta Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SRPT) and Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) for Duchenne muscular dystrophy, also became the agency’s chief medical officer and chief scientific officer recently.

Leading gene-editing drug developers Editas Medicine (NASDAQ:EDIT), Intellia Therapeutics (NASDAQ:NTLA), Beam Therapeutics (BEAM), and CRISPR Therapeutics (CRSP) were all in the green notwithstanding the latest changes at the CBER.

However, Capricor Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CAPR), whose cell therapy candidate, deramiocel, is currently under FDA priority review, traded lower. Verdun is said to be a key official tasked with overseeing the company’s marketing application for deramiocel, targeted at Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy.

data.title: Peraso regains Nasdaq compliance on minimum bid price
data.description:
• Peraso (NASDAQ:PRSO) received notification from Nasdaq that it has regained compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.
  • The company's stock maintained a closing bid price above $1 for 10 consecutive trading days as of June 17, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: CarMax sells more vehicles in Q1, driving profits up despite lower car prices
data.description:
Fueled by a 9% rise in used car sales that helped offset softer vehicle prices, CarMax (NYSE:KMX) delivered a double-digit earnings increase and a 6.1% gain in net sales—both topping Wall Street expectations and sending shares up more than 10% in early Friday trading.

For the first quarter ended May 31, CarMax (NYSE:KMX) earned a profit of $1.38 per share, up 42.3% from a year ago and $0.21 better than expected. While wholesale gross profit per unit was down $17 per unit, retail gross profit increased by $60 per unit to $2,407. In addition, the company earned $9 more per unit on each Extended Protection Plan sold, as well as a $128 per unit improvement in service margin per unit.

Regarding the company’s sales, 379,727 retail and wholesale vehicles were sold during the quarter, an increase of 5.8% from last year. This resulted in a 6.2% increase in total sales to $7.55B, $20M more than expected.

The company bought 288K vehicles from consumers and 48K through dealers, resulting in a combined increase of 7.2% from a year ago. Average selling prices were down, however, with used car prices 1.5% lower and wholesale vehicle prices down 1.7%.

data.title: Jeffs’ Brands and Impact Acquisitions gets TSX Venture Exchange nod for Fort Products’ merger
data.description:
• Jeffs' Brands (NASDAQ:JFBR)  announced Friday that the TSX Venture Exchange has approved the previously disclosed acquisition by Impact Acquisitions of 100% of the equity in Fort Products, a U.K.-based wholly owned subsidiary of Jeffs’ Brands.
  • In return, Jeffs’ Brands will receive an initial 75.02% stake in Impact, with the potential to increase to 83.29% based on the achievement of certain milestones.
  • Upon completion of the proposed transaction, Impact will issue 100,000,000 common shares to the company at a deemed price of C$0.171246 per share, representing a total implied consideration of approximately C$17.1 million.

data.title: Centrus Energy wins DoE contract extension to continue HALEU production
data.description:
Centrus Energy (NYSE:LEU) +3.7% pre-market Friday after saying the U.S. Department of Energy exercised an option to extend its contract for producing high-assay, low-enriched uranium through June 30, 2026; the DoE has additional options for continued production as many as eight additional years.

The one-year extension, valued at ~$110M, continues the partnership the DoE launched with Centrus (NYSE:LEU) in 2019 to restore U.S. ability to enrich uranium and provide a source of HALEU.

Centrus (LEU) completed Phase 1 of a three-phase contract in late 2023 by launching enrichment operations and delivering 20 kg of HALEU, and Phase 2, which concludes June 30, 2025, the company is producing an additional 900 kg of HALEU for the DoE's use.

The DoE amended the contract this month, splitting the first three-year extension period into a one-year option followed by a two-year option; the department has exercised the first of these options, kicking off Phase 3I with additional HALEU production through June 30, 2026.

The remaining options in the contract would, if exercised, provide for up to eight additional years of production beyond the current extension, the company said.
